informal: Show that the collection $\{(a,b) \mid a < b, a \text{ and } b \text{ rational}\}$ is a basis that generates the standard topology on $\mathbb{R}$.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_13_8a :
  IsTopologicalBasis {S : Set ℝ | ∃ a b : ℚ, a < b ∧ S = Ioo ↑a ↑b}
reasoning: The informal statement asks to show the collection of rational open intervals (a,b) with a<b rational is a basis generating the standard topology on ℝ. The Lean statement uses `IsTopologicalBasis` applied to the set of `Set ℝ` of the form `Ioo ↑a ↑b` for rationals a<b. In Mathlib, `IsTopologicalBasis` is defined relative to the ambient topology on the type, and ℝ carries the standard topology by default, so this asserts both that the collection is a basis and that it generates the standard topology. The encoding `∃ a b : ℚ, a < b ∧ S = Ioo ↑a ↑b` correctly captures rational endpoints with a<b. This matches the informal claim.
